overview: Dredge is a graveyard-centric combo deck that mills itself and opponents to accumulate a large graveyard, then uses Dread Return to cast Stinkweed Imp from the graveyard. Once the Imp enters, it triggers repeatedly to mill the opponent's library and drain life, while Generous Ent provides alternative lethal damage based on graveyard interactions. The deck leverages W/B/R/G mana to maximize mill synergy and recursion within Pauper's legal card pool.
win_conditions: Cast Stinkweed Imp from the graveyard using Dread Return to mill the opponent's library and drain life
Use Generous Ent to deal lethal damage as cards are put into graveyards
Accumulate graveyard resources to repeatedly cast threats and outvalue opponents

keep_criteria: Hands containing Dread Return paired with early mill spells
Hands featuring Generous Ent alongside cheap graveyard fillers
Hands with multiple low-cost spells to enable turn 1-2 graveyard setup
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably duals or shocklands, or basic Swamps/Forests), at least one dredge enabler (Dredge, Stinkweed Imp, or similar), and a recursion engine or disruption spell.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking Dread Return or mill synergy
Hands with only high-cost cards that stall development
Hands with no way to interact or fill the graveyard
Mulligan any hand with 0-1 lands, all non-basic lands with no fetches, or hands with only disruption and no graveyard synergy.

early_game: Play lands and cheap mill spells to fill your graveyard
Prioritize drawing into Dread Return and Stinkweed Imp
Use Generous Ent early if possible to apply pressure
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a dredge enabler or disruption spell.
Turn 2: Play a second land, cast another dredge enabler or recursion piece.
If you have multiple dredge cards, chain them to fill the graveyard quickly.
Prioritize establishing a graveyard engine over board presence.
mid_game: Cast Dread Return to bring back Stinkweed Imp
Trigger Imp repeatedly to mill opponent and drain life
Use Generous Ent's ability to deal damage as cards are milled
Mana should be hoarded for recursion spells and responding to threats.
Cards in hand should be used for disruption early, then saved for protection.
The graveyard is your primary resource
avoid unnecessary discards and prioritize filling it with high-value targets (Griselbrand, Nexus of Fate, Bitter Ordeal).
Dredge has minimal board presence early.
Commit threats only when you can protect them or when you're close to winning.
late_game: Maintain board presence with recursive threats
Use graveyard recursion to outvalue opponents
Close out games with repeated Imp triggers or Ent damage
Griselbrand (drain life), Nexus of Fate (infinite turns/draws), Bitter Ordeal (infinite combat/draws), Dread Return (reanimate threats).
Win by chaining recursion to generate lethal damage or infinite turns.
Go all-in when you have a recursion engine in play, enough mana to cast it, and the opponent has no immediate graveyard hate or removal.
If you can resolve a win condition and have backup recursion, commit fully.

key_cards.name: Generous Ent
key_cards.role: Primary win condition and pressure piece
key_cards.notes: Cast early to deal damage as you mill yourself and opponents; keep it on board to maximize cumulative damage

key_cards.name: Stinkweed Imp
key_cards.role: Core combo piece and mill engine
key_cards.notes: Cast from graveyard via Dread Return; trigger its ability repeatedly to mill opponent's library and drain life

key_cards.name: Dread Return
key_cards.notes: Use to cast Stinkweed Imp from graveyard; sequence after milling enough cards to ensure Imp can be cast and triggered

sequencing_tips: Mill yourself before casting Dread Return to ensure Stinkweed Imp is in the graveyard
Trigger Stinkweed Imp immediately after it enters to maximize mill before opponent can respond
Hold up Generous Ent's ability if needed to deal damage after opponent's graveyard is depleted
Prioritize mill spells that don't require targets to avoid interaction
Dredge is highly dependent on graveyard synergy
avoid playing cards that don't contribute to it.
Be mindful of turn-based actions and priority when chaining dredge effects.
Watch for opponent's sideboard cards that target the graveyard.
Practice sequencing to maximize recursion efficiency.
Common mistake: overcommitting to a single win condition without backup recursion.

matchups.advice: Race by casting Generous Ent early and using mill to deal damage; prioritize Dread Return on turn 3-4
Mill aggressively to bypass counterspells; use Dread Return on empty stack or after opponent commits resources
Apply pressure with Generous Ent while setting up graveyard; use Imp triggers to outvalue their curve
Use discard to remove counterspells from hand. Cast dredge enablers and recursion spells at instant speed when possible. Use cheap, resilient threats that don't rely on a single resolution.
Apply early pressure with dredge engines. Use discard to remove ramp spells. If they outpace you, rely on graveyard recursion to out-value their mana advantage.

Win the graveyard war. Prioritize discarding their dredge enablers and recursion pieces. Use Tormod's Crypt or Surgical Extraction to remove their key cards. Control the graveyard with hate cards and win by out-recursing them.

common_threats: Watch for graveyard hate (Rest in Peace, Leyline of the Void, Tormod's Crypt, Surgical Extraction).
Track opponent's hand for counterspells or removal.
Note when they start playing removal or hate, as it signals they've identified your game plan.
